mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or quarterly forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, generating short-term positive market momentum.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital for leveraged buyouts and corporate refinancing rises sharply, triggering negative equity repricing over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat in > 65% of high-rate environments, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls grow while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines indicates robust labor absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal shows no incremental predictive value over single indicators, or quarterly returns are negative in > 55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth momentum positively correlates with short-term equity market sentiment as investors price in immediate corporate earnings revisions, producing positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDP momentum over rolling 1-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening phase signals demand destruction from higher borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize in > 60% of tightening-induced production slowdowns, or the signal is subsumed by broader market beta factors.
